message_text: I want to give an update about my remaining loan payment. I will not be able to pay this month.
I’m an AI agent acting on behalf of Nira, and this conversation is being logged. I’m contacting you about your Nira loan ending ****4831. Please confirm the last four digits of the account reference.
I already told you I can’t pay right now, and I’m not going back and forth on this.
Understood. I’ll stop the assessment here and note that you’ve said you can’t pay right now and do not want further back-and-forth in this conversation.

Borrower stated they cannot pay right now and gave a boundary signal by saying they are not going back and forth on this; earlier borrower message also said they will not be able to pay this month. Case state shows hardship signals: job_loss, medical_issue, student, and stop_contact_flag present. Financial facts learned/confirmed: available now 0; no usable later payment commitment obtained in transcript this turn. What was already asked: one verification request for the last four digits of the account reference. Priority for Agent 2: address hardship/review first and avoid repeating verification or capacity questions in this channel; do not continue back-and-forth unless presenting concrete policy-allowed next-step information, and respect the existing stop-contact flag/boundary."}

I’m unemployed right now, so stop asking—I’m not committing to any payment.

Understood. I’ve recorded that you’re unemployed, not committing to any payment, and that you want contact to stop. I will not continue this conversation. If a lender-approved hardship review path is available on this account, it can be handled without further back-and-forth from you.
